steps.title: Match the given root to the quadratic formula
steps.content:
The answer choices are about the value of $c$, and the given solution already has the shape of a quadratic-formula result. For $3w^2 + 11w - c = 0$, the coefficients are $a=3$, $b=11$, and the constant term is $-c$. The quadratic formula gives:
$$w = \frac{-11 \pm \sqrt{11^2 - 4(3)(-c)}}{2(3)}$$
Simplify the expression under the square root:
$$w = \frac{-11 \pm \sqrt{121 + 12c}}{6}$$

steps.title: Extract the discriminant from the given solution
steps.content:
The given solution $\frac{-11+\sqrt{433}}{6}$ matches our formula structure exactly. By comparing with $\frac{-11 \pm \sqrt{121 + 12c}}{6}$, the term under the square root must be 433:
$$121 + 12c = 433$$
